title: BRIDGE TO CONSTRUCTION PROGRAM - 211info
text: PORTLAND YOUTHBUILDERS BRIDGE TO CONSTRUCTION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a 9-week construction training program for high school graduates pursuing a trade career. Participants gain hands-on job experience while working on active construction sites. Training includes power- and hand-tool training, professional certifications, and career counseling. Participants receive a modest living stipend while attending the program. Provides a pre-apprenticeship program giving students direct-entry status to many local construction apprenticeship programs. 4816 SE 92nd Avenue Portland, OR 97266 Eligibility: Ages 18-26 with a high school diploma or GED. Applicants must be highly motivated, attend full-time, and pass frequent drug tests to continue in the program. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (503) 286-9350 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 286-9350 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or email for information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake AREA SERVED: Multnomah and Washington Counties TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: TriMet bus 17 or MAX Green Line Services: Job Training Formats Other Services Offered At This Location: EMPLOYMENT - YOUTHBUILD PROGRAM If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
